channel.title: bash on hermanl0
channel.description: Recent content in bash on hermanl0

item.title: mtr,whois,bgpq3
item.description: these are some useful commands when gathering information on different IPs and AS numbers mtr (my traceroute) mtr -z -t 8.8.8.8 --report > mtr_out.txt less mtr_out.txt Start: 2024-04-24T15:06:28+0200 ... 13. AS15169 dns.google whois whois AS15169 ASNumber: 15169 ASName: GOOGLE bgpq3 user@ubuntu:~/Downloads$ bgpq3 -4 15169 ip prefix-list NN permit 8.8.4.0/24 ip prefix-list NN permit 8.8.8.0/24
item.content:encoded:
these are some useful commands when gathering information on different IPs and AS numbers

mtr (my traceroute)

mtr -z -t 8.8.8.8 --report > mtr_out.txt 

less mtr_out.txt 

Start: 2024-04-24T15:06:28+0200  
...
13. AS15169  dns.google

whois

whois AS15169

ASNumber: 15169
ASName:         GOOGLE

bgpq3

user@ubuntu:~/Downloads$ bgpq3 -4 15169

ip prefix-list NN permit 8.8.4.0/24 
ip prefix-list NN permit 8.8.8.0/24

item.title: basic firewalld commands
item.description: basic firewalld commands firewall-cmd --get-active-zones firewall-cmd --list-ports firewall-cmd --state
item.content:encoded:
basic firewalld commands

firewall-cmd --get-active-zones
firewall-cmd --list-ports
firewall-cmd --state

item.title: date loop
item.content:encoded:
date loop

while true;do clear;date;sleep 1;done

item.title: encode and decode with base64
item.description: base64 -i example-config.txt > base64-encoded.txt more base64-encoded.txt Y29uZmlnIHRleHQK base64 -d -i base64-encoded.txt > base64-decoded.txt more base64-decoded.txt config text
item.content:encoded:
base64 -i example-config.txt > base64-encoded.txt
more base64-encoded.txt
Y29uZmlnIHRleHQK

base64 -d -i base64-encoded.txt > base64-decoded.txt
more base64-decoded.txt
config text

item.title: adding ssh keys to github
item.description: adding ssh key to github SSH Setup: on your Ubuntu machine, generate a new SSH key pair by running the following command in your terminal: ssh-keygen -t rsa -b 4096 -C "your_email@example.com" once generated, add your new key to the ssh-agent: eval "$(ssh-agent -s)" ssh-add ~/.ssh/id_rsa copy your SSH public key: cat ~/.ssh/id_rsa.pub add key to github clone the repository with the command: git clone git@github.com:YourUserName/YourRepoName.git replace “YourUserName/YourRepoName.git” with your Github username and the repository name.
item.content:encoded:
adding ssh key to github

SSH Setup:

on your Ubuntu machine, generate a new SSH key pair by running the following command in your terminal:

ssh-keygen -t rsa -b 4096 -C "your_email@example.com"

once generated, add your new key to the ssh-agent:

eval "$(ssh-agent -s)"
ssh-add ~/.ssh/id_rsa

copy your SSH public key:

cat ~/.ssh/id_rsa.pub

add key to github

clone the repository with the command:

git clone git@github.com:YourUserName/YourRepoName.git

replace “YourUserName/YourRepoName.git” with your Github username and the repository name.

item.title: setting timezone in linux
item.description: setting timezone in linux timedatectl set-timezone your_time_zone
item.content:encoded:
setting timezone in linux

timedatectl set-timezone your_time_zone
